title: Supreme Court Rules: You Can't Sue Roundup for Cancer
script: Your weed killer won? The Supreme Court ruled 7-to-2 today that you cannot sue Bayer — makers of Roundup — for failing to warn you about cancer risk from its main chemical, glyphosate. John Durnell used Roundup for years and got non-Hodgkin lymphoma, won a $1.25 million Missouri jury verdict, and brought it all the way to the Supreme Court. Gone. The court ruled the EPA said glyphosate was safe and that federally-approved labels preempt state-level lawsuits. Bayer stock jumped nearly 19 percent. The company still faces roughly 60,000 remaining lawsuits on other grounds. Glyphosate is in virtually every American lawn. The International Agency for Research on Cancer still lists it as a probable human carcinogen. So if you're still using Roundup, who's responsible if something goes wrong?
